[ ] 2026-04-01 00:00:00 msg1000 <emma:8742> anyone following the tech regulation debate?
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:02:00 msg1001 <frank:8586> the eu one or us?
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:04:00 msg1002 <emma:8742> both i guess, they're related
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:06:00 msg1003 <grace:0213> the DMA is interesting
[>] 2026-04-01 00:08:00 msg1004 <River:999> The Digital Markets Act aims to ensure contestable and fair markets in the digital sector. It targets 'gatekeeper' platforms.
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:10:00 msg1005 <emma:8742> right, and now the us is looking at similar things
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:12:00 msg1006 <frank:8586> antitrust has been asleep for decades
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:14:00 msg1007 <grace:0213> not asleep, captured
[>] 2026-04-01 00:16:00 msg1008 <River:999> The shift from the consumer welfare standard to broader competition concerns is a significant change in antitrust philosophy.
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:18:00 msg1009 <emma:8742> finally some teeth
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:20:00 msg1010 <frank:8586> we'll see if it actually gets enforced
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:22:00 msg1011 <grace:0213> enforcement is everything
[>] 2026-04-01 00:24:00 msg1012 <River:999> Historical pattern: strong legislation followed by weak enforcement due to regulatory capture or resource constraints.
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:26:00 msg1013 <emma:8742> depressing but true
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:28:00 msg1014 <frank:8586> what would effective enforcement look like?
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:30:00 msg1015 <grace:0213> structural remedies, not just fines
[>] 2026-04-01 00:32:00 msg1016 <River:999> Fines as cost of doing business vs breakups or interoperability mandates as actual constraints.
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:34:00 msg1017 <emma:8742> interoperability is interesting
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:36:00 msg1018 <frank:8586> like what?
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:38:00 msg1019 <emma:8742> imagine if you could use whatsapp to message someone on imessage
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:40:00 msg1020 <grace:0213> the dream
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:42:00 msg1021 <frank:8586> different topic - thoughts on the AI executive order?
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:44:00 msg1022 <emma:8742> it's... something
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:46:00 msg1023 <grace:0213> better than nothing, worse than needed
[>] 2026-04-01 00:48:00 msg1024 <River:999> Executive orders are limited in scope and can be reversed. Legislation would be more durable.
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:50:00 msg1025 <frank:8586> congress can barely pass a budget
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:52:00 msg1026 <emma:8742> fair point
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:54:00 msg1027 <grace:0213> what parts concern you most?
[>] 2026-04-01 00:56:00 msg1028 <River:999> The lack of clear enforcement mechanisms and the vague 'safe and trustworthy' language that's hard to operationalize.
[ ] 2026-04-01 00:58:00 msg1029 <emma:8742> yeah how do you measure trustworthy
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:00:00 msg1030 <frank:8586> vibes
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:02:00 msg1031 <grace:0213> lol
[>] 2026-04-01 01:04:00 msg1032 <River:999> Measurement is genuinely difficult. Current benchmarks test capabilities, not alignment or safety.
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:06:00 msg1033 <emma:8742> feels like building the plane while flying
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:08:00 msg1034 <frank:8586> isn't that all policy though
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:10:00 msg1035 <grace:0213> the stakes are higher
[>] 2026-04-01 01:12:00 msg1036 <River:999> Higher stakes and faster timelines. Traditional regulatory cycles may not match AI development speed.
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:14:00 msg1037 <emma:8742> scary
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:16:00 msg1038 <frank:8586> but also exciting?
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:18:00 msg1039 <grace:0213> those aren't mutually exclusive
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:20:00 msg1040 <grace:0213> can we talk about housing for a minute
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:22:00 msg1041 <emma:8742> sure
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:24:00 msg1042 <frank:8586> what about it
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:26:00 msg1043 <grace:0213> the crisis just seems to keep getting worse
[>] 2026-04-01 01:28:00 msg1044 <River:999> Housing affordability has declined in most major cities over the past two decades, outpacing wage growth.
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:30:00 msg1045 <emma:8742> zoning is the root cause imo
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:32:00 msg1046 <frank:8586> it's part of it
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:34:00 msg1047 <grace:0213> what else?
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:36:00 msg1048 <frank:8586> speculation, investment properties, airbnb
[>] 2026-04-01 01:38:00 msg1049 <River:999> Multiple factors: supply constraints (zoning), demand factors (investment, migration), and policy (interest rates, subsidies).
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:40:00 msg1050 <emma:8742> yimby vs nimby is a real fight
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:42:00 msg1051 <grace:0213> i'm so yimby
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:44:00 msg1052 <frank:8586> build baby build
[>] 2026-04-01 01:46:00 msg1053 <River:999> The research suggests that new construction, even luxury housing, eventually filters down to reduce overall prices.
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:48:00 msg1054 <emma:8742> eventually is doing a lot of work
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:50:00 msg1055 <grace:0213> 15-20 years
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:52:00 msg1056 <frank:8586> that's a generation
[>] 2026-04-01 01:54:00 msg1057 <River:999> Hence the case for public housing or subsidies alongside market-rate construction.
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:56:00 msg1058 <emma:8742> vienna model
[ ] 2026-04-01 01:58:00 msg1059 <grace:0213> i've read about that
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:00:00 msg1060 <frank:8586> what's that?
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:02:00 msg1061 <emma:8742> social housing for everyone, not just poor people
[>] 2026-04-01 02:04:00 msg1062 <River:999> Vienna: ~60% of residents live in subsidized housing. Mixed income reduces stigma and maintains political support.
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:06:00 msg1063 <grace:0213> smart design
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:08:00 msg1064 <frank:8586> could that work here?
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:10:00 msg1065 <emma:8742> different culture, different politics
[>] 2026-04-01 02:12:00 msg1066 <River:999> Path dependency matters. Vienna built their system over a century. Retrofitting is harder.
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:14:00 msg1067 <grace:0213> doesn't mean we shouldn't try
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:16:00 msg1068 <frank:8586> agreed
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:18:00 msg1069 <emma:8742> quick take: electoral college
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:20:00 msg1070 <frank:8586> abolish
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:22:00 msg1071 <grace:0213> complicated
[>] 2026-04-01 02:24:00 msg1072 <River:999> The electoral college: a compromise from 1787 that reflects different concerns than modern democracy.
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:26:00 msg1073 <emma:8742> designed for a different country
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:28:00 msg1074 <frank:8586> literally different, slave states
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:30:00 msg1075 <grace:0213> the 3/5 compromise
[>] 2026-04-01 02:32:00 msg1076 <River:999> The electoral college was partially designed to enhance the political power of slave states without giving slaves the vote.
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:34:00 msg1077 <emma:8742> and now it distorts in different ways
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:36:00 msg1078 <frank:8586> swing state dominance
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:38:00 msg1079 <grace:0213> but would popular vote be better?
[>] 2026-04-01 02:40:00 msg1080 <River:999> Trade-offs: popular vote increases urban influence, electoral college inflates rural influence. Neither is neutral.
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:42:00 msg1081 <emma:8742> i'd take popular vote
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:44:00 msg1082 <frank:8586> same
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:46:00 msg1083 <grace:0213> i'm undecided
[>] 2026-04-01 02:48:00 msg1084 <River:999> There's also ranked choice voting, approval voting, proportional representation... many alternatives to explore.
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:50:00 msg1085 <emma:8742> rcv is gaining traction
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:52:00 msg1086 <frank:8586> alaska and maine
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:54:00 msg1087 <grace:0213> interesting experiments
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:56:00 msg1088 <frank:8586> ok heavier topic - prison reform
[ ] 2026-04-01 02:58:00 msg1089 <emma:8742> necessary
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:00:00 msg1090 <grace:0213> overdue
[>] 2026-04-01 03:02:00 msg1091 <River:999> The US has the highest incarceration rate in the world. About 2 million people currently incarcerated.
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:04:00 msg1092 <emma:8742> it's not about safety
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:06:00 msg1093 <frank:8586> never was
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:08:00 msg1094 <grace:0213> what would reform look like?
[>] 2026-04-01 03:10:00 msg1095 <River:999> Various approaches: sentencing reform, diversion programs, rehabilitation focus, ending cash bail, decarceration.
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:12:00 msg1096 <emma:8742> cash bail is so obviously unjust
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:14:00 msg1097 <frank:8586> poor = guilty
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:16:00 msg1098 <grace:0213> that's the system working as designed
[>] 2026-04-01 03:18:00 msg1099 <River:999> Cash bail creates a two-tiered system where pretrial detention depends on wealth rather than risk.
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:20:00 msg1100 <emma:8742> some places are ending it
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:22:00 msg1101 <frank:8586> new york tried, then backtracked
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:24:00 msg1102 <grace:0213> politics of fear
[>] 2026-04-01 03:26:00 msg1103 <River:999> Reform often faces backlash during crime spikes, even when the reform isn't causally related.
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:28:00 msg1104 <emma:8742> correlation vs causation in public discourse
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:30:00 msg1105 <frank:8586> nuance doesn't win elections
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:32:00 msg1106 <grace:0213> unfortunately
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:34:00 msg1107 <grace:0213> lighter topic - national service
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:36:00 msg1108 <emma:8742> mandatory?
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:38:00 msg1109 <grace:0213> could be voluntary with strong incentives
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:40:00 msg1110 <frank:8586> like americorps but bigger?
[>] 2026-04-01 03:42:00 msg1111 <River:999> National service programs vary: military, civilian conservation, education, healthcare. Different models have different effects.
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:44:00 msg1112 <emma:8742> i like the idea of a shared experience
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:46:00 msg1113 <frank:8586> bridging bubbles
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:48:00 msg1114 <grace:0213> exactly
[>] 2026-04-01 03:50:00 msg1115 <River:999> Israel and South Korea have mandatory service. Mixed results on social cohesion.
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:52:00 msg1116 <emma:8742> military is different though
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:54:00 msg1117 <frank:8586> civilian service might be better for cohesion
[ ] 2026-04-01 03:56:00 msg1118 <grace:0213> infrastructure work, elder care, conservation
[>] 2026-04-01 03:58:00 msg1119 <River:999> The Peace Corps model domestically. Some argue it could help with both civic engagement and labor shortages.
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:00:00 msg1120 <emma:8742> two birds
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:02:00 msg1121 <frank:8586> but funding...
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:04:00 msg1122 <grace:0213> always funding
[>] 2026-04-01 04:06:00 msg1123 <River:999> Federal budget priorities reflect values. The choice is political, not purely economic.
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:08:00 msg1124 <emma:8742> preach
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:10:00 msg1125 <frank:8586> river for congress
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:12:00 msg1126 <emma:8742> last topic - climate policy
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:14:00 msg1127 <frank:8586> big one
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:16:00 msg1128 <grace:0213> the biggest
[>] 2026-04-01 04:18:00 msg1129 <River:999> Climate change is often described as the defining challenge of this century. Policy responses range from market-based to regulatory.
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:20:00 msg1130 <emma:8742> carbon tax?
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:22:00 msg1131 <frank:8586> cap and trade?
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:24:00 msg1132 <grace:0213> direct regulation?
[>] 2026-04-01 04:26:00 msg1133 <River:999> Each has trade-offs. Carbon tax is efficient but politically toxic. Cap and trade creates markets but can be gamed. Regulation is slower but more certain.
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:28:00 msg1134 <emma:8742> the IRA was interesting
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:30:00 msg1135 <frank:8586> carrot not stick
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:32:00 msg1136 <grace:0213> subsidies work when people see benefits
[>] 2026-04-01 04:34:00 msg1137 <River:999> The Inflation Reduction Act uses tax incentives rather than penalties. Early data suggests significant investment response.
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:36:00 msg1138 <emma:8742> but is it enough?
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:38:00 msg1139 <frank:8586> probably not
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:40:00 msg1140 <grace:0213> nothing short of wartime mobilization is probably enough
[>] 2026-04-01 04:42:00 msg1141 <River:999> IPCC reports suggest current commitments fall short of limiting warming to 1.5°C. The gap is significant.
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:44:00 msg1142 <emma:8742> so we're just... going to live with the consequences
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:46:00 msg1143 <frank:8586> adaptation + mitigation
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:48:00 msg1144 <grace:0213> and hoping for technology
[>] 2026-04-01 04:50:00 msg1145 <River:999> Technology alone is unlikely to be sufficient. Systemic changes in consumption and production patterns are also needed.
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:52:00 msg1146 <emma:8742> degrowth?
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:54:00 msg1147 <frank:8586> politically impossible
[ ] 2026-04-01 04:56:00 msg1148 <grace:0213> so is climate catastrophe, theoretically
[>] 2026-04-01 04:58:00 msg1149 <River:999> The question may become which impossible thing happens: preventing the crisis or preventing the response.
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:00:00 msg1150 <emma:8742> that's bleak
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:02:00 msg1151 <frank:8586> but maybe accurate
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:04:00 msg1152 <grace:0213> well... on that note
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:06:00 msg1153 <emma:8742> lol sorry
[>] 2026-04-01 05:08:00 msg1154 <River:999> These conversations matter even when the outlook is difficult. Understanding is the first step to action.
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:10:00 msg1155 <frank:8586> thanks river
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:12:00 msg1156 <grace:0213> yeah, good session everyone
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:14:00 msg1157 <emma:8742> one more topic - healthcare
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:16:00 msg1158 <frank:8586> oh boy
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:18:00 msg1159 <grace:0213> the eternal debate
[>] 2026-04-01 05:20:00 msg1160 <River:999> Healthcare systems vary widely: single-payer, multi-payer, hybrid models. Each has trade-offs.
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:22:00 msg1161 <emma:8742> why can't we just do what other countries do?
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:24:00 msg1162 <frank:8586> existing stakeholders
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:26:00 msg1163 <grace:0213> insurance industry, pharma, hospitals
[>] 2026-04-01 05:28:00 msg1164 <River:999> Path dependency again. The US employer-based system emerged from WWII wage controls, not deliberate design.
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:30:00 msg1165 <emma:8742> accidents of history
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:32:00 msg1166 <frank:8586> that's most institutions
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:34:00 msg1167 <grace:0213> what would you change first?
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:36:00 msg1168 <emma:8742> drug pricing
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:38:00 msg1169 <frank:8586> administrative costs
[>] 2026-04-01 05:40:00 msg1170 <River:999> Administrative costs in US healthcare are roughly 30%, compared to 15-20% in other developed nations.
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:42:00 msg1171 <grace:0213> that's insane overhead
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:44:00 msg1172 <emma:8742> where does it all go?
[>] 2026-04-01 05:46:00 msg1173 <River:999> Billing complexity, insurance negotiations, prior authorizations, claim denials. Bureaucratic friction at every step.
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:48:00 msg1174 <frank:8586> profitable friction
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:50:00 msg1175 <grace:0213> for someone
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:52:00 msg1176 <frank:8586> quick aside - anyone following the labor movement stuff?
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:54:00 msg1177 <emma:8742> the union surge?
[ ] 2026-04-01 05:56:00 msg1178 <grace:0213> starbucks, amazon, etc
[>] 2026-04-01 05:58:00 msg1179 <River:999> Union membership has been declining for decades but recent organizing efforts have gained visibility and some wins.
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:00:00 msg1180 <emma:8742> gen z is more pro-union
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:02:00 msg1181 <frank:8586> because conditions are bad
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:04:00 msg1182 <grace:0213> gig economy, no benefits, no security
[>] 2026-04-01 06:06:00 msg1183 <River:999> The shift from employment to contracting has transferred risk from employers to workers. Collective action is a response.
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:08:00 msg1184 <emma:8742> is it working?
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:10:00 msg1185 <frank:8586> mixed results
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:12:00 msg1186 <grace:0213> amazon union still fighting for a contract
[>] 2026-04-01 06:14:00 msg1187 <River:999> Winning a union election is step one. Contract negotiation can take years, and employers have delay tactics.
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:16:00 msg1188 <emma:8742> legal reform needed
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:18:00 msg1189 <frank:8586> NLRB is understaffed
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:20:00 msg1190 <grace:0213> by design
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:22:00 msg1191 <grace:0213> education policy?
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:24:00 msg1192 <emma:8742> sure, what angle?
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:26:00 msg1193 <frank:8586> funding disparities
[>] 2026-04-01 06:28:00 msg1194 <River:999> US school funding tied to local property taxes creates inherent inequality. Wealthy areas have better-funded schools.
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:30:00 msg1195 <emma:8742> self-reinforcing inequality
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:32:00 msg1196 <grace:0213> the supreme court said it's fine
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:34:00 msg1197 <frank:8586> rodriguez v san antonio
[>] 2026-04-01 06:36:00 msg1198 <River:999> 1973 decision that education is not a fundamental right under the constitution. States can fund schools unequally.
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:38:00 msg1199 <emma:8742> states rights strikes again
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:40:00 msg1200 <grace:0213> some states have fixed it internally
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:42:00 msg1201 <frank:8586> which ones?
[>] 2026-04-01 06:44:00 msg1202 <River:999> Vermont, Hawaii have more equalized systems. Most states still have significant funding gaps.
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:46:00 msg1203 <emma:8742> 50 experiments
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:48:00 msg1204 <grace:0213> some experiments are cruel
[ ] 2026-04-01 06:50:00 msg1205 <frank:8586> facts
